Daniel, you asked the question originally and then a bunch of people agreed. Do you do you want to be a more articulate so I make sure I cover this the way that you want it?"

NÚMEROS: N/A

And what this ended up resulting in was just having far too many staff, fronting payroll, having income inconsistencies because the staff weren't technically all revenue driven roles."

NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Bold emphasis on conditional hiring - only when blocked from higher-value work

[16:15] "And now listen to this. This is this is where hiring becomes so justified. Okay? If I'm taking an action that technically drives revenue, right? and I'm inhibited on my ability to take an action that could pay me more because of the current action I'm having to take."

NÚMEROS: 3 steps

[17:35] "I look at the role I'm doing. I quantify how much me doing that is producing, right? I want to know the actual dollars that it produces within a given month and I want to see it from a perspective of okay if if this was reduced by about 20 to 30%."
[17:55] "Or if this amount of revenue was reduced in half from me having somebody else do it instead of me. You guys tell me, have you ever had a staff member come in and just absolutely [__] on whatever you were doing and they do it way better?"

[1:39:10] "And this is a big issue when you think of efficiency. It's I want to add like a key word to this which I think which I think is is pretty critical which is ruthless efficiency. Okay."
[1:39:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "RUTHLESS EFFICIENCY"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Bold emphasis, core operating principle highlighted
[1:39:35] "I do not posture myself or others when it comes to retaining my time. I am in control of my time. I do not let others rob me of my time by being a [__] about it. Okay?"
[1:39:55] "If I am doing something that I don't want to do, I do not do it. I stop doing it. Okay? And I either get other people to do it or I dramatically cut down on the amount of time it takes to do it because I'm actively at all times thinking with efficiency."
[1:40:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Efficiency Definition"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Core definition - minimum input, maximum output
[1:40:25] "Efficiency is very simple to understand. To be clear, how can you put as little in as possible and get literally the same, if not more, out as as though you put in a lot, right?"
[1:40:45] "It's very possible to do this in almost all situations when you actively think of how it's done. Okay. And I constantly bring this up when I talk about efficiency."
[1:41:05] "I always relate it to Elon Musk because I genuinely don't believe that there's a better example on Earth currently, like literally that we can all look at because the man is alive operating at the exact same time that we're all doing what we're doing right now. Okay?"
[1:41:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study: Elon Musk"

[1:41:55] "Like he had he had people he pays a lot of money to, like several hundred,000 a year that probably also have vested stock options, okay?"
[1:42:15] "That tell him like it was physicists, mathematicians, engineers, and [__] rocket scientists that when he builds those rockets try to tell him the longest possible time frames."
[1:42:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Expert Estimates vs Musk Demands"

[2:22:00] "Like all those transcripts, they go into the content finderbot, too. And so there's some kind of like indexing system that I don't even know how the [__] it works,"
[2:22:20] "but like she'll take the transcripts and she'll she'll go back and forth with Gelani on like indexing all that [__] So you guys have access to all that content. Same thing with new SOPs and things like that."
[2:22:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Technical Coordination"
NÚMEROS: N/A

[2:22:45] "Um, all kinds of random [__] dude. Like there's so much more than that, too. But those are some of the ones that come top of mind."
[2:23:00] "Is it the kind of thing where like for like a task, are you doing it once, making an SOP and then giving it to her or is she just like figuring stuff out and then she"
[2:23:20] "That's the best part about about Athena. Like they train and hire like you'd have to do that with a with a normal like Philippines VA, but like these these people are extensively well trained from Athena."

[2:23:45] "So no, like I don't have to teach her how to do pretty much anything. Like she'll she'll proactively figure it out. I just I just tell her the outcomes that we want. Got it."

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Staff atual: 12 pessoas (todas pagam por si mesmas)
Custo EA Athena: $3K/mês
Custo SEO/Blog: $5K/mês ($60K/ano) - posição de "gamble"
Office Manager: $4K/mês (consolidou $8K em custos dispersos)
Percentual aceitável de gamble: 5-10% do payroll total
Exemplo John: $35-40K/mês em 5 horas/semana = $1,750/hora
Alternativa vendas John: $144K potencial em 20 horas (40% close, $18K AOV) = $7,200/hora
Exemplo Andrew: $511K/mês com 4.5x ROAS (sem calls semanais)
Exemplo Daniel: $100K/mês MRR em 32 horas/mês = $3,125/hora (÷3 pessoas = $1,041/hora real)
Meeting compression: 2 horas → potencial 20-30 minutos
Elon Musk exemplo: 10 anos estimados → 8 meses entregues (92% redução)
Salário premium: $10-15K/mês atrai top talent
Private aviation: 2+ horas economizadas por viagem
Trophies mastermind: 5 total (2 para $5M/mês, 3 para $1M/mês)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Regra de Ouro do Hiring:
Staff deve pagar por si mesmo via revenue production OU time savings
Time savings só se justifica se libera você para atividades de maior valor/hora
5-10% do payroll pode ir para "gamble positions" (investimentos de longo prazo)
Framework Dollar Value Per Hour:
Auditar TODAS as atividades e categorizá-las por $/hora
Priorizar ruthlessly as atividades de maior valor
Abandonar ou delegar atividades de baixo valor
Itemizar ao invés de agrupar (ex: "running ads" ≠ "writing copy" ≠ "building funnels")
Hiring Decision Framework:
Quantificar receita produzida pela atividade atual
Calcular horas mensais investidas
Determinar $/hora da atividade
Projetar conservadoramente (50% performance de replacement)
Calcular payroll que ainda gera ROI positivo
Avaliar o que você faria com tempo liberado
Revenue Gap Management:
Aceitar períodos temporários de perda/investimento
Usar tempo liberado para fechar o gap o mais rápido possível
Focar em atividades que desbloqueiam maior valor futuro
Ruthless Efficiency Principles:
Acreditar genuinamente que pode ser feito em menos tempo
Impor time constraints agressivos
Comunicação em bullet points ("tweets")
Eliminar casualidade e repetição
Accountability cultural top-down
Questionar necessidade de meetings (async vs sync)
Communication Hierarchy:
Mensagem > Call de 10 min > Call de 30 min > Call de 1+ hora
Real-time response para problemas urgentes
Structured meetings apenas quando absolutamente necessário
Elon Musk Efficiency Model:
Recusar estimativas iniciais
Forçar compressão de timeline
Substituir processos caros por simulações
Papel do líder = accountability para eficiência
Questionar constantemente "why not faster?"
ESTRUTURA:
Tier de Atividades (mais alto para mais baixo):
Atividades de $7,000+/hora (ex: sales calls de alto ticket)
Atividades de $3,000-5,000/hora (ex: running ads com rev share, content creation)
Atividades de $1,000-2,000/hora (ex: fulfillment calls que geram referrals)
Atividades de $500-1,000/hora (ex: team coordination que retém revenue)
Atividades de $0-500/hora (candidatas a delegação)
Atividades negativas/zero (eliminar ou consolidar em role multi-purpose)
Tipos de Payroll Positions:
Revenue Production:
Sales reps
Media buyers
Account managers que retêm MRR
Time Savings:
EAs (Athena) - $3K/mês
Marketing helpers (funnels, automation, zaps)
Office managers - $4K/mês
Gamble Positions (5-10% budget):
SEO/Content - $5K/mês
Experimental marketing channels
Long-term brand building
Premium Talent ($10-15K/mês):
Highly trained
Self-starters
Cultural fit
Top wage earner pool
Can produce 50-70% of founder output
Meeting Structure Optimization:
Weekly standups: avaliar necessidade
Formato bullet point obrigatório
Time constraints explícitos e acreditados
Async quando possível (Slack/Telegram)
Calls apenas para decisões complexas
Athena EA Capabilities:
Video transcription + time coding
Translation (Spanish)
SOP library creation
Contractor coordination
AI system integration support
Personal errands
Content processing
Outcome-driven (não precisa de micro-management)
AI Leverage Examples:
Ad copy via LLM (60 min → 5 min)
Training material as AI context
Manis agent mode para replicar expertise
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
ROI Calculations:
John's onboarding: $35K/20hrs = $1,750/hr
John's sales potential: $144K/20hrs = $7,200/hr (4.1x multiplier)
Daniel's calls: $100K/32hrs = $3,125/hr solo, $1,041/hr com 3 pessoas
Office manager: $4K/mês vs $8K em contractors = 50% savings
EA Athena: $3K/mês (inclui $1,500 detailing + múltiplas outras tasks)
Payroll Budget Allocation:
90-95%: Self-funding positions (revenue or verified time savings)
5-10%: Gamble positions (strategic long-term bets)
0%: Pure cost centers (exceto office manager que consolida custos)
Break-Even Examples:
Blog SEO: 1 student/mês cobre $5K investment
Office manager: Immediate savings vs scattered contractors
EA: Múltiplos time savings que liberam founder para revenue activities
Premium Hiring Math:
$10-15K/mês salary
Expectativa: 50% founder performance conservatively
Ex: $35K activity → $20K with hired talent = $15K reduction
Founder freed time: $7,200/hr activity = $144K/mês gain
Net: -$15K + $144K = +$129K upside
Time Compression Value:
2hr meeting → 20min = 100 min saved/week = 400 min/month
At $1,041/hr rate = ~$7,000/mês saved just by format change
Zero cost implementation (belief + structure change)
Private Aviation ROI:
2+ hours saved per trip
Immediate productivity (work on laptop)
Justifies cost when $/hour activities exceed flight premium
DURAÇÃO: 2h 27min FORMATO: Group coaching call via Zoom, screen-share com exemplos visuais e coaching ao vivo com múltiplos participantes (Daniel, John, Andrew)
